message:
Add pending rotations fetch to Init and integrate PT health states

- Add fetchPendingRotations() call to dashboard Init for immediate data loading
- Integrate process triage health states with BuildPaneTableRows
- Add PTHealthStatesMsg type and refreshPTHealth constant
- Add healthStates field to Model for pane health tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"ntm-l3hh","title":"Add user confirmation option for rotation","description":"## Overview
Allow users to require confirmation before auto-rotation.

## Location
Extend `internal/context/rotation.go`

## Configuration
```toml
[context_rotation]
require_confirmation = false  # Default: auto-rotate without asking
confirmation_timeout_sec = 60 # How long to wait for response
```

## Confirmation Flow
When rotation is triggered and confirmation required:

1. **Pause new work** to the agent
2. **Show notification** in dashboard:
   ```
   ┌─ Rotation Required ────────────────────────┐
   │ Agent cc_1 (architect) is at 85% context   │
   │                                            │
   │ Options:                                   │
   │   [R] Rotate now                           │
   │   [C] Try compact first                    │
   │   [I] Ignore (continue as-is)              │
   │   [P] Postpone 30 minutes                  │
   │                                            │
   │ Auto-rotate in: 45s                        │
   └────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   ```
3. **Wait for response** or timeout
4. **Execute choice** or default action

## Robot Mode Output
```json
{
  "type": "rotation_pending",
  "agent_id": "cc_1",
  "context_percent": 85.2,
  "awaiting_confirmation": true,
  "timeout_seconds": 45,
  "options": ["rotate", "compact", "ignore", "postpone"]
}
```

## CLI Override
```bash
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=rotate
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=compact
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=ignore
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=postpone --minutes=30
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Configuration option
- [ ] Dashboard notification UI
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts for options
- [ ] Timeout with default action
- [ ] Robot mode pending notification
- [ ] CLI confirmation command
- [ ] Tests for confirmation flow","notes":"Progress: Created rotation_confirm.go dashboard panel with keybindings (r=rotate, c=compact, i=ignore, p=postpone). Added tests (all passing). Integrated panel initialization and message type into dashboard.go. Remaining: Wire up message handling and data fetching.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-31T02:43:18.488611467Z","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:15:10.108354488Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-l3hh","depends_on_id":"ntm-cj0w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l3hh","depends_on_id":"ntm-jv8p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-l4fp","title":"Add Agent Mail notifications for scan results","description":"## Overview
Integrate UBS scan results with Agent Mail to notify coordinating agents about code quality findings, enabling proactive collaboration on fixes.

## Background
When multiple AI agents work on a codebase, scan results are valuable coordination signals:
- New critical findings need immediate attention
- Findings in files an agent is editing should be surfaced
- Scan summaries help agents prioritize work

## Implementation Details

### Notification Types
1. **Scan Summary** - After each scan, send digest to all active agents
   - Total findings by severity
   - New vs. existing findings
   - Files with most issues

2. **Targeted Alerts** - Notify specific agents about relevant findings
   - Match findings to file reservations
   - Agent editing auth.go gets auth-related findings
   - Importance: high for critical, normal for others

3. **Fix Confirmations** - When findings are resolved
   - Notify original reporter
   - Update thread if findings were discussed

### Message Templates
```markdown
Subject: [Scan] {n} new findings in {project}

## Scan Summary
- Critical: {critical_count}
- Errors: {error_count}  
- Warnings: {warning_count}

## New Findings
{finding_list}

## Your Files
The following findings affect files you're working on:
{agent_relevant_findings}
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/scanner/notify.go - Notification logic
- internal/scanner/matcher.go - File reservation matching
- internal/cli/scan.go - Add --notify flag

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scan summaries sent to registered agents
- [ ] Findings matched to file reservations
- [ ] Appropriate importance levels set
- [ ] Notifications are concise and actionable","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T08:33:31.765440Z","updated_at":"2025-12-10T21:56:58.898130Z","closed_at":"2025-12-10T21:56:58.898130Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-l4fp","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l4fp","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-l5h","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure","description":"# EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure

## Background & Rationale

NTM (Named Tmux Manager) currently has very low test coverage across most packages. This EPIC addresses the need for:

1. **Full Unit Test Coverage** - Testing each function/method in isolation WITHOUT mocks or fakes where possible, using real dependencies
2. **Integration Tests** - Testing component interactions (CLI → tmux, config → commands, etc.)
3. **End-to-End (E2E) Tests** - Full workflow tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Current Coverage Analysis

| Package | Current Coverage | Status |
|---------|-----------------|--------|
| cmd/ntm | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/cli | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/config | 16.2% | 🟡 Needs improvement |
| internal/output | 55.1% | 🟡 Good start |
| internal/palette | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/robot | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/status | 69.6% | 🟢 Good (has failures) |
| internal/tmux | 5.4% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tui/* | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tutorial | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/updater | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/watcher | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |

## Testing Philosophy

### No Mocks/Fakes Policy
- Use REAL tmux sessions for tmux tests (create/destroy per test)
- Use REAL file system for config tests
- Use REAL HTTP calls where applicable
- Only mock external services that are impractical (GitHub API rate limits, etc.)

### Detailed Logging Strategy
- All E2E tests output structured logs with timestamps
- Log file per test run with full command output
- Capture stderr/stdout separately
- Include system state before/after each test

## Success Criteria
- 80%+ coverage on all non-TUI packages
- 60%+ coverage on TUI packages (harder to test without mocks)
- All E2E tests pass on macOS and Linux
- Tests complete in <5 minutes total
- No flaky tests (3 consecutive passes required)

## Technical Considerations
- Tests requiring tmux must be skipped in CI without tmux
- Use Go's testing.Short() for quick vs full test modes
- Parallelize where safe (different tmux sessions)
- Clean up resources even on test failure","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T08:17:38.345103Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T03:38:02.593734Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T03:38:02.593734Z","close_reason":"All children completed: Unit Test Coverage (ntm-idx), Integration Tests (ntm-7lk), E2E Tests (ntm-vr2), spawn tests (ntm-gnyw). Comprehensive testing infrastructure in place.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ntm-l5y","title":"Integrate beads into robot snapshot","description":"Include beads project status in unified snapshot.

## Rationale
Beads is how we track work. The snapshot needs to include bead summary so orchestrator knows what work is available, blocked, and in-progress.

## Integration Points
1. Detect if .beads/ directory exists
2. Run bd stats or equivalent query
3. Include summary counts and optionally ready list

## Snapshot Extension
```json
{
  "...": "existing fields",
  "beads": {
    "project": "/path/to/project",
    "summary": {
      "total": 58,
      "open": 45,
      "in_progress": 3,
      "blocked": 15,
      "ready": 27,
      "closed": 13
    },
    "ready_preview": [
      {"id": "ntm-1rz", "title": "Create profiler package", "priority": "P0"},
      {"id": "ntm-7mg", "title": "Add design tokens", "priority": "P1"}
    ],
    "in_progress": [
      {"id": "ntm-abc", "title": "Implement feature X", "assigned": "BlueLake"}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Ready Preview Limit
- Default 5 items
- --robot-snapshot --bead-limit=10 to change
- Sorted by priority then age

## Graceful Degradation
```json
{"beads": {"available": false, "reason": "no .beads/ directory"}}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenCastle","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:53:26.959482Z","updated_at":"2025-12-10T08:58:54.711742Z","closed_at":"2025-12-10T08:58:54.711742Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
